question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: systemEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit crafted configuration input that overrides the `shard` or `realm` values in `CommonProperties`, causing `SystemEntity.toEntityId()` to map system file IDs (101, 102, 111, 112) to wrong entity IDs, thereby reorganizing which transactions are indexed as address-book or fee-schedule updates in the mirror node history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: systemEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of upper-bound validation on `CommonProperties.shard` and `CommonProperties.realm` (only `@Min(0)` is enforced) to inject arbitrarily large shard/realm values, causing `SystemEntity.toEntityId()` to produce entity IDs that collide with user-owned accounts, reorganizing transaction history attribution?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: systemEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a race condition on the `@Getter(lazy = true)` fields in `SystemEntity` (e.g., `addressBookFile101`, `feeScheduleFile`) during concurrent Spring context refresh, causing two different `EntityId` values to be cached for the same system entity, leading to inconsistent transaction history classification?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: hibernatePropertiesCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user abuse the `GENERATE_STATISTICS = true` Hibernate setting to extract query execution metadata through a timing side-channel, inferring the internal ordering of transaction history records and then crafting targeted inputs to disrupt that ordering?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: hibernatePropertiesCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `hibernate.criteria.literal_handling_mode = BIND` setting by crafting bind parameter values that, when stored in JSONB columns via `CustomJsonFormatMapper`, produce JSON structures that alter the sort order of transaction history queries downstream?
